methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Aepnus Technology is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Lukas Hackl and Bilen Akuzum that has raised $10.6M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable set spanning 9 private frontier-tech players in Climate & Energy (including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems) plus 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10.6M raised as a proxy base valuation and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $26M. Peer-median analysis references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—which produce a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M. The peer group spans established venture-backed competitors at Series A stage.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $88M. The wide spread reflects material tension between stage-based progression ($26M) and peer multiples ($150M), indicating sensitivity to (1) whether comparables trading at Series A represent realistic near-term outcomes for Aepnus or inflated frontier-tech valuations, and (2) the company's technology risk and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort. Additional downside risk stems from frontier-tech sector volatility and execution uncertainty at Seed stage.
